mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 260 in CPIAUCSL triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 260 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in INDPRO (industrial production) and HOUST (housing starts) signal weakening supply-side capacity, creating deflationary pressures that boost real equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 20d returns in supply contraction scenarios are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3pp monthly increase in UNRATE (unemployment rate) signals deteriorating labor market conditions, reducing corporate earnings visibility and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE rises by 0.3pp despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 140k in the prior month indicates robust demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 for any 2-year window, or 20d returns in high PAYEMS environments are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 10bp increase in FEDFUNDS combined with rising CPIAUCSL creates a 'stagflation' environment, reducing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.1pp and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise by the specified thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDPC1 (GDP) signals economic contraction, reducing corporate earnings and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 for any 3-year window, or 60d returns in GDP contraction scenarios are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% monthly increase in INDPRO (industrial production) indicates strong supply-side momentum, supporting corporate margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO rises by 2% despite the signal.
